sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Separator bar had no effect. Let me try the small green and yellow squares.
# Also try the Y container frame and separator.
# First, let me get full segmentation to identify all clickable objects.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("All nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:3]}...")

# Try clicking the small green square (node 0) - likely at top
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 8, 'col': 30}])
print("\nClicked small green square (8, 30):")
print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg_after = current_frame.segmentation
n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
y_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg_after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
print(f"N pixels: {n_pixels}")
print(f"Y pixels: {y_pixels}")

if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    # Try small yellow square
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 28, 'col': 10}])
    print("\nClicked small yellow square (28, 10):")
    print("Result:", last_action_result)
    
    seg_after2 = current_frame.segmentation
    n_pixels2 = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg_after2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'}
    y_pixels2 = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in seg_after2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y'}
    print(f"N pixels: {n_pixels2}")
    print(f"Y pixels: {y_pixels2}")
</parameter>
